question.text: Which of the following is an Officially Valid Document (OVD) under PML Rules for Proof of Identity?

answer.text: All of these -- Passport, Driving Licence, Aadhaar, Voter's ID, NREGA Job Card, NPR Letter
Only Voter's ID and Driving Licence

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** OVDs under Rule 2(d) of PML Rules include: (i) passport, (ii) driving licence, (iii) proof of Aadhaar number, (iv) Voter's ID, (v) NREGA job card signed by a State Government officer, (vi) NPR letter with name and address, and (vii) any other document notified by Central Government.
**The Trap:** PAN is NOT an OVD under PML Rules, though it is mandatory separately. The OVD list is specifically defined and includes six categories plus a residual category. Many candidates incorrectly think only Aadhaar and Passport qualify.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The OVD list is identical across NISM V-A, III-A, and this exam. Knowing all six categories is essential as questions test whether candidates can identify what IS and what is NOT an OVD.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Accept any of the six OVDs from clients. Some rural clients may have only NREGA job cards -- these are fully valid OVDs. For simplified measures, additional documents like government ID cards with photos are also acceptable.
